finding_results.notes: Hermione's threshold beat now carries specific bodily strain through the cuff check, throat pulse, and involuntary orientation toward the west side of the house. finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_02.md:193; chapters/chapter_02.md:199; chapters/chapter_02.md:203; chapters/chapter_02.md:217; chapters/chapter_02.md:221; chapters/chapter_02.md:229
finding_results.notes: Six targeted edits across the negotiation scene (lines 193-229). Hermione now self-corrects mid-diagnostic at 193 ('but I can't — that's a surface read') and over-specifies her boundary at 217 ('I'm not promising treatment — I'm not even promising a full diagnostic'). Draco's proposal at 199 truncates before naming the outcome he wants, and at 203 he starts toward a personal admission and pulls back to institutional framing ('And I'm not — the Ministry is not an option'). Hermione's research-priority condition at 221 adds a narrowing clause ('around it, not the other way round') and her records condition at 229 uses contractions consistent with her high contraction level. The parallel if-then chain in the boundary-setting speech is reduced from three clauses to two with the Department folded as a parenthetical narrowing. Terms remain fully legible; voices now separate under pressure.

finding_results.notes: Compressed the opening Manor inventory into action-driven route choice and reduced the winter-house setup to details that feed Draco's avoidance and the later corridor reveal. Tightened the returned-corridor sequence so the spatial detail now points at the house shortening Hermione's route to the library and study rather than restating atmosphere.

pass_summaries.summary: Revised Chapter 2 into a fuller three-scene confrontation chapter: Draco's isolation and preparation now carry more causal weight, Hermione's return to the Manor and the Mark negotiation land with concrete bodily and war-history pressure, Narcissa's residence is corrected to Knightsbridge, and the chapter now clears the 6000-word floor while preserving the planned end state of terms set and the Manor opening the corridor without Draco reporting it.

Single finding addressed: the Mark-negotiation dialogue (lines 193-235) was revised to break the composed-contract cadence. Hermione's voice now shows pressured self-correction and over-specification; Draco's shows verbal pull-back and semantic incompleteness. Contraction level aligned to style bible (Hermione high, Draco moderate-formal under stress). No structural, continuity, or setup/payoff changes; all must-land beats and the corridor-opening setup preserved intact.

Revised the catalogue-style Manor description into motive-bearing scene work while preserving the chapter's engine: Draco's isolation, the exact professional negotiation, the restless Mark, and the Manor's silent response to Hermione's arrival.
